title: MVP Data Mapping Properties Side Panel
story: As a data migration specialist, I want a slide-out panel where I can input my source data schema and map fields to Salesforce objects, so that I can perform data mapping without manually entering each field individually.
description:
Implement the MVP version of the Data Mapping properties panel as an off-canvas (slide-out) side panel. This is the foundational UX for the data mapping workflow.

**MVP scope** is limited to:
- The slide-out panel UX (off-canvas from the right side)
- Ability to add a new data source using the Handsontable-based spreadsheet UX
- Saving the data source schema to the application's data model
- Only UX Object 1 from the full specification — additional mapping objects are deferred

The panel allows users to define their source system's field structure, which becomes the basis for field-level mapping between source and Salesforce fields.

After customer feedback, the panel was moved from left-side to right-side so users can still see all field names in the main table while the panel is open.
acceptance_criteria: Off-canvas slide-out panel opens from the right side of the screen
User can add a new data source with field definitions using the spreadsheet UX
Source data schema is saved to the application data model
Panel does not obscure the field name column in the main data table
Panel can be opened and closed without losing data
ux_changes: New right-side off-canvas slide-out panel with Handsontable-based spreadsheet for entering source data field definitions. Panel slides in from the right to keep field names visible.
middleware_changes: Backend logic to process and validate source data schema submissions from the panel.
data_model_changes: New data model entities to store source data schemas, including source object definitions and field mappings.
api_changes: Endpoints to create, save, and retrieve source data schemas for the mapping panel.
implementation_notes: Initially scoped as a backlog item but elevated to a sprint priority as the last major feature for beta launch. The panel was changed from left-to-right orientation based on customer feedback so users could still see all field names. Uses the Handsontable library for the spreadsheet-like data entry experience.
comment_summary: Developer initially asked for clarification on which page needed the off-canvas panel. It was clarified as a backlog item. Later, it was updated with detailed requirements and elevated to sprint priority for beta launch. Customer feedback prompted moving the panel from left to right side, which was a quick change.
